content.full_text:
June 3, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
428 J Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN: Lynda Cassady

Dear Lynda:

This firm represents the State Council of Laborers. I am writing to confirm telephone advice I received from you concerning the reporting obligations of the State Council of Laborers under the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.

The State Council of Laborers registered as a lobbyist employer for the first time for the 1991-1992 Legislative Session commencing December 3, 1990. The State Council has two in-house lobbyists.

The State Council filed its first Lobbyist Employer Report (Form 635) for the fourth quarter of 1990. Even though the State Council did not register until December 1990 and the lobbyists did not meet the contacts or compensation tests until December 1990, the report covered the entire fourth quarter of October 1, 1990 through December 31, 1990.

Included on the report of Chuck Center, one of the in-house lobbyists, was an activity expense paid on October 31, 1990. By letter of May 20, 1991, the Secretary of State indicated that registration was required for the 1989-1990 Legislative Session apparently based on the reporting of this activity expense.

You confirmed that the reporting of the activity expense was required since the report covered the entire period; however, registration for the 1989-1990 Legislative Session was not required since the lobbyists did not qualify as lobbyists until December 1990. You also indicated that you would call the Secretary of State and inform them of this advice.

Lynda Cassady  
June 3, 1991  
Page 2

Please contact me right away if I have stated the advice incorrectly. Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]

DIANE M. FISHBURN  
DMF/ccz  
cc: Barbara Smith, Secretary of State's Office  
Chuck Center
